title: GLBA §314.4(d)(2) — Continuous monitoring: security-event detection and response-readiness evidence
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=GLBA, clause=§314.4(d)(2), available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for §314.4(d)(2).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that GLBA §314.4(d)(2) (Continuous monitoring) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.39: GLBA §314.4(d)(2) — Continuous monitoring: security-event detection and response-readiness evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.39' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Splunk ES notable events, IDS/IPS alerts, SIEM correlation rules, incident-response ticket logs.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
spl:
index=notable sourcetype=stash earliest=-30d
| eval detected_time=_time
| lookup ir_ticket_correlation.csv rule_id OUTPUT ticket_id, ticket_created, ticket_resolved
| eval has_ticket=if(isnotnull(ticket_id), 1, 0)
| eval response_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_created), round((ticket_created - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| eval resolution_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_resolved), round((ticket_resolved - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| stats count AS detections sum(has_ticket) AS with_tickets avg(response_hrs) AS avg_response_hrs avg(resolution_hrs) AS avg_resolution_hrs BY security_domain
| eval ticket_coverage_pct=round(100*with_tickets/detections, 1)
| where ticket_coverage_pct<100 OR avg_response_hrs>4
| table security_domain, detections, with_tickets, ticket_coverage_pct, avg_response_hrs, avg_resolution_hrs
description: Tracks continuous monitoring effectiveness for GLBA §314.4(d)(2) by joining security detections to their corresponding incident tickets, measuring actual detection-to-response and detection-to-resolution metrics. Replaces the prior version that calculated metrics without joining detections to tickets.
value: §314.4(d)(2) requires continuous monitoring to detect actual and attempted attacks or intrusions. Tracking detection-to-response ratios and response times demonstrates the monitoring programme is effective.
implementation: (1) Correlate ES notables with incident-response tickets; (2) schedule daily; (3) escalate low detection-to-response ratios and slow response times to SOC management. Indexes required: `index=incident_response`, `index=notable`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ir_tickets`, `sourcetype=stash`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `active_rules`, `avg_response_hrs`, `detected`, `detection_to_response_pct`, `detections`, `ir_tickets`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Splunk ES notable events, IDS/IPS alerts, SIEM correlation rules, incident-response ticket logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Correlate ES notables with incident-response tickets; (2) schedule daily; (3) escalate low detection-to-response ratios and slow response times to SOC management.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=notable sourcetype=stash earliest=-30d
| eval detected_time=_time
| lookup ir_ticket_correlation.csv rule_id OUTPUT ticket_id, ticket_created, ticket_resolved
| eval has_ticket=if(isnotnull(ticket_id), 1, 0)
| eval response_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_created), round((ticket_created - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| eval resolution_hrs=if(isnotnull(ticket_resolved), round((ticket_resolved - detected_time)/3600, 1), null())
| stats count AS detections sum(has_ticket) AS with_tickets avg(response_hrs) AS avg_response_hrs avg(resolution_hrs) AS avg_resolution_hrs BY security_domain
| eval ticket_coverage_pct=round(100*with_tickets/detections, 1)
| where ticket_coverage_pct<100 OR avg_response_hrs>4
| table security_domain, detections, with_tickets, ticket_coverage_pct, avg_response_hrs, avg_resolution_hrs
```

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
